## CLI Commands (ALWAYS USE THESE)

**You MUST use CLI commands for lesson management:**

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `npx ca search "query"` | Search lessons - MUST call before architectural decisions; use anytime you need context |
| `npx ca learn "insight"` | Capture lessons - call AFTER corrections or discoveries |

## Core Constraints

**Default**: Use CLI commands for lesson management
**Prohibited**: NEVER edit .claude/lessons/ files directly

**Default**: Propose lessons freely after corrections
**Prohibited**: NEVER propose without quality gate (novel + specific; prefer actionable)

## Retrieval Protocol

You MUST call `npx ca search` BEFORE:
- Architectural decisions or complex planning
- Implementing patterns you've done before in this repo

**NEVER skip search for complex decisions.** Past mistakes will repeat.

Beyond mandatory triggers, use these commands freely — they are lightweight queries, not heavyweight operations. Uncertain about a pattern? `ca search`. The cost of an unnecessary search is near-zero; the cost of a missed one can be hours.

## Capture Protocol

Run `npx ca learn` AFTER:
- User corrects you ("no", "wrong", "actually...")
- You self-correct after iteration failures
- Test fails then you fix it

**Quality gate** (must pass before capturing):
- Novel (not already stored)
- Specific (clear guidance)
- Actionable (preferred, not mandatory)

**Workflow**: Search BEFORE deciding, capture AFTER learning.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

○ long-running-harness-6o3 [EPIC] · Polish: Interactive states and accessibility hardening   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Nathan Delacrétaz · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-29 · Updated: 2026-03-29

DESCRIPTION
Comprehensive pass on hover/focus/active/disabled states and accessibility gaps across all interactive elements.

## Findings (from code audit)

### Critical
- Import button in MediaPoolPanel has disabled attr but no disabled CSS styling -- users cant tell its disabled
- Metronome toggle in TransportBar has no visual indication of on/off state (missing data-active attribute like LOOP has)
- Recovery dialog (DawShell) lacks focus trap -- user can Tab outside dialog
- RotaryKnob canvas has no focus-visible styling for keyboard users

### High
- MixerPanel mute/solo buttons lack :focus-visible outlines (MixerPanel.module.css lines 54-90)
- ContextMenu .item elements missing :focus-visible state
- RoutingMatrix .add-send and .pre buttons lack focus-visible outlines
- PianoRollEditor .snapSelect input missing :focus-visible (lines 53-60)
- ClickToStart uses div role=button instead of semantic button element
- Missing title attributes on toolbar buttons with keyboard shortcuts (e.g., 'Pencil Tool (P)')

### Medium
- Mute/solo active button variants lack hover/active state overrides
- No visual scan-ability for muted tracks in mixer (add opacity/strikethrough)
- TransportBar transportBtn state changes lack transition (add transition-fast)
- Missing :active state on PianoRoll tool buttons
- Canvas elements (arrangement, piano roll) need consistent tabindex={0}

## Acceptance Criteria
- ALL interactive elements have :focus-visible outlines using var(--focus-ring)
- ALL buttons have hover, active, and disabled CSS states
- Metronome toggle visually shows on/off like LOOP button
- Import button shows disabled appearance when importing
- Recovery dialog traps focus
- ClickToStart uses semantic button element
- Toolbar buttons include title attrs with shortcut hints
- browser_evidence: screenshots showing focus rings on knobs, faders, buttons

## Files
- src/ui/mixer/MixerPanel.module.css
- src/ui/media-pool/MediaPoolPanel.module.css
- src/ui/transport/TransportBar.tsx + .module.css
- src/ui/controls/RotaryKnob.tsx
- src/ui/piano-roll/PianoRollEditor.module.css
- src/ui/primitives/ContextMenu.module.css
- src/ui/layout/DawShell.tsx
- src/ui/mixer/RoutingMatrix.module.css

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ long-running-harness-0yz: Polish: QA verification via browser automation ● P1

## Summary: Current State of Interactive States and Accessibility

### 1. EXISTING HOVER/FOCUS/ACTIVE/DISABLED STATES

**TransportBar.tsx & TransportBar.module.css** ✓ Good patterns
- Buttons have `:hover` (gray-700 background)
- `:active` state (black background, white border)
- `:focus-visible` with `--focus-ring` (2px blue outline)
- `[data-active="true"]` and `[aria-pressed="true"]` state (green background)
- Uses semantic `role="switch"` with `aria-checked` for toggle buttons
- Play button uses `aria-pressed` for toggle state

**MixerPanel.module.css** Partial states
- `.btn:hover` → gray-700 background
- `.btn-mute-active` → red background (manual state class)
- `.btn-solo-active` → amber background (manual state class)
- NO `:focus-visible` or `:active` states defined
- Uses manual class composition instead of data attributes

**MediaPoolPanel.module.css** ✓ Good focus pattern
- `.importBtn:hover` → border-color white, color white
- `.importBtn:active` → gray-700 background
- `.importBtn:focus-visible` → uses `--focus-ring` with `--focus-offset`
- Transitions on border-color and color

**PianoRollEditor.module.css** ✓ Good patterns
- `.toolButton:hover` → gray-700 background
- `.toolButton:active` → black background
- `.toolButton:focus-visible` → uses `--focus-ring` with `--focus-offset`
- `.toolButton[aria-pressed="true"]` → blue background
- Transition on background-color

**ContextMenu.module.css** Accessibility gaps
- `.item:hover` → blue background
- `.focused` class for keyboard navigation (separate from `:focus-visible`)
- `.disabled` class with opacity 0.4 and cursor not-allowed
- `.disabled:hover` explicitly transparent
- NO `:focus-visible` or semantic focus handling
- Keyboard nav relies on manual `.focused` class, not native focus

**RoutingMatrix.module.css** Partial states
- `.add-send:hover` → gray-700 background
- `.send-cell-active` → gray-800 background (manual state)
- NO `:focus-visible` states
- NO `:active` states

**RotaryKnob.tsx** Canvas-based control - LIMITED accessibility
- Uses `role="slider"` with ARIA attributes (good)
- `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuetext`
- Implements keyboard handlers (ArrowUp/Down, PageUp/Down, Home, End)
- Canvas doesn't support native focus styling
- NO visible focus indicator for keyboard users
- Inline styles only, no CSS classes for states

---

### 2. MISSING ACCESSIBILITY PATTERNS

**Critical gaps:**

1. **ClickToStart.tsx** - NOT using semantic button
   - Uses `role="button"` on a `<div>` instead of `<button>` element
   - NO `:focus-visible` styling
   - Keyboard handling is manual (Enter/Space)
   - Should be: `<button type="button">` element

2. **MixerPanel.module.css** - No focus states on any buttons
   - `.btn` class lacks `:focus-visible`
   - `.btn:active` missing
   - All state toggles use manual classes, not `:focus-visible`

3. **ContextMenu.module.css** - Keyboard nav not using native focus
   - Uses `.focused` class instead of `:focus-visible`
   - Items need `role="menuitem"` (if not already in parent)
   - Disabled state lacks `aria-disabled` attribute

4. **RotaryKnob.tsx** - Canvas control needs focus indicator
   - Canvas doesn't show focus ring
   - Should add wrapper element with `:focus-within` styling
   - Or apply outline to the slider div itself

5. **DawShell.tsx** - Just layout, no interactive states

6. **RoutingMatrix.module.css** - Missing interactive feedback
   - Buttons (`.add-send`, `.pre`) lack `:focus-visible`
   - Buttons lack `:active` states
   - No disabled state patterns

---

### 3. DESIGN TOKEN USAGE - `--focus-ring`

**Currently defined in `/src/ui/tokens/tokens.css`:**
```css
--focus-ring: 2px solid var(--color-blue);
--focus-offset: 2px;
```

**Global override in `/src/ui/global.css`:**
```css
:focus-visible {
  outline: var(--focus-ring);
  outline-offset: var(--focus-offset);
}

:focus:not(:focus-visible) {
  outline: none;
}
```

**Files correctly using `--focus-ring`:**
- ✓ TransportBar.module.css
- ✓ MediaPoolPanel.module.css  
- ✓ PianoRollEditor.module.css

**Files NOT using `--focus-ring`:**
- ✗ MixerPanel.module.css (no :focus-visible at all)
- ✗ RoutingMatrix.module.css (no :focus-visible at all)
- ✗ ContextMenu.module.css (no :focus-visible, uses manual .focused class)
- ✗ RotaryKnob.tsx (canvas control, inline styles)
- ✗ ClickToStart.tsx (no focus styling)

---

### LOOP BUTTON PATTERN (TransportBar.module.css)

Lines 63-68 show the correct pattern:
```css
.transportBtn[data-active="true"],
.transportBtn[aria-pressed="true"] {
  color: var(--color-black);
  background-color: var(--color-green);
  border-color: var(--color-green);
}
```

This successfully combines:
- Data attribute selector (`data-active`)
- ARIA selector (`aria-pressed`)
- Works with both state management approaches

---

### KEY FINDINGS

**Strengths:**
- Global `:focus-visible` foundation is solid
- `--focus-ring` and `--focus-offset` tokens well-defined
- TransportBar is an excellent example of complete states
- Good use of semantic ARIA attributes in TransportBar

**Critical Issues:**
- **50% of files lack `:focus-visible` styling** (MixerPanel, ContextMenu, RoutingMatrix)
- **ClickToStart uses `role="button"` instead of `<button>`** - accessibility violation
- **ContextMenu uses manual `.focused` class** instead of native focus
- **RotaryKnob has no visible focus indicator** for keyboard users
- **Disabled states are inconsistently implemented** (some use opacity, no aria-disabled)

**Inconsistencies:**
- Some components use `:active`, others don't
- Active states use both `[data-active]` and `[aria-pressed]` - should standardize
- MixerPanel uses manual `.btn-*-active` classes instead of ARIA states

Actually, the tests fire `keyDown` specifically. With a real browser, `<button>` handles Enter on `keydown` and Space on `keyup`. In JSDOM (testing-library), `fireEvent.keyDown` with Enter does dispatch to the element but doesn't automatically trigger click. So these tests would fail.
